title: Hardened Keys
description: Hardened derivation is the BIP32 child-key mode that prevents a leaked child private key plus parent xpub from revealing the parent private key. It creates a security boundary inside HD wallet trees at the cost of giving up public-only derivation below that branch.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Wallets
resources.notes: Canonical walkthrough of hardened derivation and why it changes what can be derived from extended public keys.

resources.title: BIP 32: Hierarchical Deterministic Wallets
resources.notes: Canonical specification for hardened and non-hardened child derivation.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Broad technical reference for HD wallet trees, xpub/xprv boundaries, and practical derivation tradeoffs.
